quick_facts.label: Service model
quick_facts.value: An Agniveervayu is enrolled in the Indian Air Force (IAF) under the Air Force Act, 1950, for a four-year engagement including training. The role is a distinct rank, and the IAF is not obliged to retain the person after the four-year term.

quick_facts.label: Latest cited intake context
quick_facts.value: The IAF’s Agniveervayu Intake 02/2027 notice set online registration for 6–26 July 2026 and the online examination for 22–23 September 2026. Dates, vacancies, eligibility and selection procedures can change with each intake notification.

quick_facts.label: Education eligibility example
quick_facts.value: For Intake 02/2027, science-subject candidates needed qualifying 10+2, engineering diploma, or specified vocational credentials involving Physics and Mathematics; other-than-science candidates needed qualifying 10+2 in any stream or a qualifying vocational course. The notice generally required at least 50% aggregate and 50% in English; applicants must rely on the notification for their own intake.

quick_facts.label: Regular-airman pathway
quick_facts.value: After four years, Agniveervayu may apply for enrolment into the regular IAF airmen cadre. Selection is centralised, performance-based and subject to organisational requirements; up to 25% of a specific batch may be enrolled, with no entitlement to selection.

quick_facts.label: Pay and exit package context
quick_facts.value: The Intake 02/2027 notice lists a customised monthly package beginning at ₹30,000 in year 1 and rising to ₹40,000 in year 4, with 30% contributions to the Agniveer Corpus Fund and matching Government contributions. It states an approximately ₹10.04 lakh Seva Nidhi corpus at exit before applicable interest; package, allowances and interest are notification- and rule-dependent.

quick_facts.label: Benefits and limitations
quick_facts.value: During engagement, the cited notice provides for service-hospital medical facilities, CSD provisions, applicable allowances and a non-contributory ₹48 lakh life-insurance cover. It also states that there is no entitlement to gratuity, pensionary benefits or ex-servicemen status after the four-year engagement.

quick_facts.label: Education while serving
quick_facts.value: IGNOU offers five skill-based bachelor’s programmes exclusively for Agniveers of the Army, Navy and Air Force. Under the scheme, 50% of credits are supplied through the respective Armed Force, alongside IGNOU coursework.

sections.heading: Role and responsibilities
paragraphs: Agniveervayu is a four-year IAF service role rather than a conventional permanent airman appointment. On enrolment, personnel receive military training based on IAF requirements and may be assigned any duty in organisational interest at the IAF’s discretion. The precise trade, technical exposure and daily duties therefore depend on service requirements rather than the candidate’s examination stream alone.

The broader Agnipath framework describes the service period as building military skills and experience, discipline, fitness, leadership and courage. The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship describes Agniveer service as comprising basic military training, trade training, security training, and service/on-the-job training.
bullets: Undertake IAF-directed military and role-specific training.
Remain deployable for duties assigned in organisational interest.
Develop documented skills and competencies; the IAF notice provides for a detailed skill-set certificate at the end of engagement.

sections.heading: Entry route: Agniveervayu selection
paragraphs: The direct route is through an IAF Agniveervayu intake notification and the designated online recruitment portal. The cited Intake 02/2027 notification invited unmarried Indian male and female candidates, with the number and employability of female candidates determined by service requirements. Its age, education, medical and domicile rules are intake-specific and should be checked afresh in the live notification.

For Intake 02/2027, selection began with an online Phase-I objective test. Science candidates were tested in Physics, Mathematics and English; other-than-science candidates were tested in English plus Reasoning and General Awareness. Shortlisted candidates proceeded to Phase II, subject to document verification and the remaining prescribed selection and medical processes.
bullets: Use only the official IAF recruitment channel named in the current notification; do not pay intermediaries or agents.
Check the notification’s date-of-birth block, qualification and English-mark requirements before applying.
Prepare for both academic testing and prescribed physical/medical standards; the notification makes the medical board’s decision conclusive.
Keep original education, identity and domicile/COAFP documents ready for later stages.

sections.heading: Skills developed and recognised
paragraphs: The role combines military, trade, security and on-the-job learning. Government reporting states that the Directorate General of Training has mapped Agniveer training and operational roles to civilian job roles and competencies aligned with nationally approved qualification frameworks; this is intended to support formal recognition of skills and transitions into civilian work.

For Agniveers with the required prior qualification, IGNOU’s defence-personnel scheme offers applied-skills bachelor’s programmes. The programmes combine discipline coursework with credits for Armed Forces’ in-service skill education, creating a route to complete a degree while serving.
bullets: Military discipline, physical fitness, teamwork and leadership.
Technical or operational trade learning determined by IAF assignment.
Security and workplace readiness through supervised service/on-the-job training.
Skills documentation through the end-of-engagement Agniveer Skill Certificate.

sections.heading: Workplace and service conditions
paragraphs: Agniveervayu serve within the IAF under the Air Force Act, 1950. The cited recruitment notice states that personnel must be physically and mentally fit to perform duty in any part of the world, in any climate and terrain, reflecting the role’s mobility and operational demands.

The four-year engagement has service-specific conditions. The cited Intake 02/2027 notice specifies 30 days of annual leave per year, with sick leave based on medical advice; it also says grant of leave is subject to IAF exigencies. It requires candidates to be unmarried at enrolment and includes conditions relating to marriage during the engagement period.
bullets: Posting location, work environment and duties are determined by IAF organisational requirements.
Leave and release before completion are controlled by service rules; voluntary early release is generally not permitted except in exceptional cases approved by the competent authority.
Medical facilities at service hospitals and CSD provisions apply for the engagement period under the cited notice.

sections.heading: Progression after the four-year engagement
paragraphs: The internal progression route is application for the regular IAF airmen cadre at the end of the four-year term. It is competitive rather than automatic: up to 25% of each batch may be selected centrally on objective criteria including performance, subject to organisational requirements and Government discretion. Those selected move to the regular cadre under the applicable service conditions, with a further minimum engagement of 15 years stated in the central Agnipath policy release.

For those leaving after four years, official policy identifies recognised skills, education and civilian transition as core pathways. A March 2026 government reply says skill sets are being aligned to civilian roles and that employment opportunities have been provided across Central Government, state governments and defence public-sector undertakings; it also identifies Seva Nidhi support and preference for loans for MSME establishment. These are enabling measures, not guarantees of a particular civilian job.
bullets: Apply for regular IAF airman enrolment, where eligible and selected.
Complete an IGNOU applied-skills bachelor’s degree during service, where eligible.
Use recognised skills and certificates in applications for public-sector, security, technical, transport or other civilian roles.
Explore entrepreneurship or MSME finance options after exit, subject to the lender’s and scheme’s current terms.

sections.heading: Compensation and benefits context
paragraphs: Compensation should be read from the intake-specific notification. For Intake 02/2027, the customised monthly package was listed as ₹30,000, ₹33,000, ₹36,500 and ₹40,000 for years 1 through 4 respectively. The notice describes a 70% in-hand component and a 30% individual contribution to the Agniveer Corpus Fund, matched by the Government; applicable risk and hardship, dress and travel allowances, plus ration, clothing, accommodation and LTC, are governed by prevailing IAF rules.

The same notice estimates a ₹10.04 lakh Seva Nidhi package after four years before interest, based on the stated corpus contributions. It explicitly states no entitlement to gratuity or pensionary benefits. Applicants should treat figures as dated context: future notifications, interest rates, tax rules and allowance eligibility may differ.
bullets: 2026 Intake 02/2027 starting monthly package: ₹30,000, with a stated annual increment.
2026 Intake 02/2027 exit corpus: approximately ₹10.04 lakh before applicable interest after four years.
Non-contributory life-insurance cover stated in the notice: ₹48 lakh during engagement.
No pension, gratuity or post-discharge ex-servicemen status under the cited notice.
